key_insights.insight: Hope, resilience, and self-determination are crucial tools for navigating adversity, and they can be cultivated even in the most restrictive environments.
key_insights.quote: Something started to grow in me that made me become the person that I am today... hope and resilience and determination and this ability not to allow someone else to dictate who you are.

key_insights.insight: The media can be a powerful tool for justice and change if understood and leveraged effectively.
key_insights.quote: I studied a correspondence journalism course in prison because I knew I needed the media to tell you and other people on the outside world that I was innocent.

key_insights.insight: The absence of intentional malice doesn't negate responsibility for harmful actions.
key_insights.quote: I can understand things, but I can't understand why someone would tell a lie that destroys somebody else's life deliberately in the way that they did mine.

key_insights.insight: Personal experiences, even traumatic ones, can be transformed into skills and tools for navigating the world and helping others.
key_insights.quote: I learned a skill where I read the character of men for so long in such an intimate way that it does put me in a position of survival when I go into these prisons.

key_insights.insight: Empathy is crucial for understanding and addressing the root causes of crime and the needs of incarcerated individuals.
key_insights.quote: There are plenty of people that you can judge for what they've done but in my space you know criminal justice prisons um not everybody in there is a bad person some people have just made mistakes.

key_insights.insight: It is essential to focus on rehabilitation and reintegration efforts for prisoners, including providing them with skills and education to lead productive lives after release.
key_insights.quote: I set up the foundation off the back of the things that I witnessed in these prisons with an intention to try and improve the opportunities for prisoners, staff,and the conditions in prison.

key_insights.insight: It's important to acknowledge and address the psychological scars that result from wrongful imprisonment, such as difficulty making choices and fear of being open with others.
key_insights.quote: There has been this innate fear in me over the years I was in prison and when I first got out of prison...I just couldn't make a decision for myself.

actionable_advice.advice: Study your environment to better understand how you can create change.
actionable_advice.how_to: Raphael studied how the media worked, so that he could plant stories in newspapers challenging issues to do with prison.

actionable_advice.advice: If you're a parent, make it a point to understand the life your child is leading to ensure you can give them the help and support that they need.
actionable_advice.how_to: When Raphael went to Jamaica, he went there to see what his dad's life was like and to understand the circumstances that made him who he was.

actionable_advice.advice: If you are struggling to make small decisions in your life, actively work to make a choice between two or more options that are available to you.
actionable_advice.how_to: Since being imprisoned, Raphael struggled to decide between simple choices like which chocolate bar to buy, you can actively choose between two options, like happy baked beans or Heinz baked beans.

memorable_stories: The chaplain in prison who spied on prisoners having intimate visits with their wives or girlfriends through a hole in the wall.
The story of Raphael connecting with his wife after not seeing her for over a decade and getting her number on a train platform after she had visited him.
contrarian_claims: Forgiveness is just a word and it doesn't necessarily mean someone truly means it. Forgiveness does not stop a person from becoming who they want to be.
Prison doesn't always make someone a bad person, many inmates are there due to one bad choice or circumstance.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Man Wrongfully Convicted Of A Murder He Didn’t Commit: Raphael Rowe
